ORDER GRANTING MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS-DEFENDANTS' "OBJECTIONS AND REPLY TO TENTATIVE DECISION" OF PROPOSED JUDGMENT AND DIRECTING PREPARATION OF PROPOSED JUDGMENT (DOUGLASS-D608)

THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT CROSS COMPLAINANT BERGER, ALEC MERZHERITSKY, NELLY RECOVER FROM CROSS DEFENDANT VARUM, GARY S. TELESIS ENGINEERS $2,077,500.75 PRINCIPAL, TOTAL JUDGMENT $2,077,500.75, ORDERED, ADJUDGED AND DECREED THAT ON THE BERGERS' BREACH OF CONTRACT AND PROFESSIONAL NEGLIGENCE CAUSES OF ACTION IN THEIR SECOND AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT, JUDGMENT IS ENTERED IN FAVOR OF THE BERGERS AND AGAINST TELESIS AND AGAINST GARY VARUM, BOTH INDIVIDUALLY AND AS AN ALTER EGO OF TELESIS, FOR ECONOMIC DAMAGES IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,577,500.75, FOR WHICH TELESIS AND GARY VARUM ARE JOINTLY AND SEVERALLY LIABLE. ON THE BERGERS' PROFESSIONAL NEGLIGENCE CAUSE OF ACTION IN THEIR SECOND AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT, JUDGMENT IS ENTERED IN FAVOR OF THE BERGERS AND AGAINST TELESIS AND AGAINST GARY VARUM, BOTH INDIVIDUALLY AND AS AN ALTER EGO OF TELESIS, FOR NON-ECONOMIC DAMAGES IN AN ADDITIONAL AMOUNT OF $500,000.00, FOR WHICH TELESIS AND GARY VARUM ARE JOINTLY AND SEVERALLY LIABLE. THE BERGERS ARE TO TAKE NOTHING BY WAY OF THEIR SECOND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT AGAINST ALEX VARUM. THE JUDGMENT IS ENTERED IN FAVOR OF THE BERGERS AND AGAINST GARY VARUM ON GARY VARUM'S FIRST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT. GARY VARUM IS TO TAKE NOTHING BY WAY OF HIS FIRST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT. SEE SCANNED DOCUMENT.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT PATRICK MCHUGH DBA MCHUGH CONSTRUCTION'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT JUDGMENT IS N DENIED. CCP SECTION 473.5 PERMITS THE COURT TO SET ASIDE A DEFAULT OR DEFAULT JUDGMENT AGAINST A DEFENDANT, IF THE DEFENDANT SHOWS AT LEAST THE FOUR FOLLOWING FACTS IN HIS MOTION: (1) HE RECEIVED, THROUGH NO INEXCUSABLE FAULT OF HIS OWN, NO ACTUAL NOTICE OF THE ACTION IN TIME TO APPEAR AND DEFEND, AND HAD NOT MADE A GENERAL APPEARANCE IN THE ACTION. (2) A DEFAULT JUDGMENT HAD BEEN ENTERED AGAINST HIM BY THE COURT. (3) HE ACTED WITH REASONABLE DILIGENCE IN SERVING AND FILING THE NOTICE OF MOTION TO SET ASIDE THE DEFAULT OR DEFAULT JUDGMENT (AND IN NO EVENT WAS THE NOTICE OF MOTION SERVED AND FILED LATER THAN THE EARLIER OF (A) TWO YEARS AFTER THE ENTRY OF A DEFAULT JUDGMENT AGAINST HIM OR (B) 180 DAYS AFTER SERVICE ON HIM OF WRITTEN NOTICE OF THE DEFAULT OR THE DEFAULT JUDGMENT). (4) THAT HE HAS A MERITORIOUS DEFENSE. (CCP SECTION 473.5 JUDICIAL COUNCIL COMMENTS.) HERE, DEFENDANT PATRICK MCHUGH HAS NOT PRESENTED SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE TO ESTABLISH THAT HE DID NOT RECEIVE NOTICE OF THE LAWSUIT. DEFENDANT MCHUGH DOES NOT PRESENT EVIDENCE TO SUPPORT HIS CLAIM THAT THE SERVICE PROCESSOR'S DECLARATION, WHICH WAS FILED WITH THE PROOF OF SERVICE OF SUMMONS, WAS FRAUDULENT. ADDITIONALLY, DEFENDANT MCHUGH DID NOT ESTABLISH, IN HIS MOVING PAPERS, THAT HE HAS A MERITORIOUS DEFENSE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. ON THE BERGERS' PROFESSIONAL NEGLIGENCE CAUSE OF ACTION IN THEIR SECOND AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT, JUDGMENT IS ENTERED IN FAVOR OF THE BERGERS AND AGAINST TELESIS AND AGAINST GARY VARUM, BOTH INDIVIDUALLY AND AS AN ALTER EGO OF TELESIS, FOR NON-ECONOMIC DAMAGES IN AN ADDITIONAL AMOUNT OF $500,000.00, FOR WHICH TELESIS AND GARY VARUM ARE JOINTLY AND SEVERALLY LIABLE. THE BERGERS ARE TO TAKE NOTHING BY WAY OF THEIR SECOND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT AGAINST ALEX VARUM. THE JUDGMENT IS ENTERED IN FAVOR OF THE BERGERS AND AGAINST GARY VARUM ON GARY VARUM'S FIRST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT. GARY VARUM IS TO TAKE NOTHING BY WAY OF HIS FIRST AMENDED CROSS-COMPLAINT. calendar.matters:
MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
Off Calendar Jun-06-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

The following matters were called concurrently: Line 1 CGC-07-462479 Vladiir Mirov vs. Arkady Berger and Line 6 CGC-14-542126 Arkady Berger vs. Gary Varum.

Appearances: Ronald Losch of Losch & Ehrlich telephone number 415-956-8400 for and with Arkady Berger.

Mr. Berger explained why he is opposed to Mr. Losch withdrawing. The Court conferred with the parties in private to avoid prejudicing Mr. Losch's rights and ruled as follows:

In re: Line 1 CGC-07-462479 Vladiir Mirov vs. Arkady Berger - Attorney Ronald Losch's MOTION TO BE RELIEVED AS COUNSEL OF RECORD for DEFENDANT ALEC BERGER is granted. Order signed in open Court.

In re: Line 6 CGC-14-542126 Arkady Berger vs. Gary Varum - PLAINTIFF ARKADY BERGER'S MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD is continued to June 21, 2016 at 9:30 a.m., in Dept. 302. The Court on its own motion on shortened time will hear a motion to vacate or, in the alternative, continue the trial, and the discovery deadline to June 21, 2016. Said motion is to be filed by June 8, 2016.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion By Cross-Complainants Alec Berger And Nelly Merzheritsky For Redetermination Of Amount Of Attorney Fees
Off Calendar May-04-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Adrian Driscoll of Murphy Pearosn Bradley & Feeney telephone number 415-788-1900 for Cross-Defendants Alec Berger and Nelly Merzheritskaya.
Adam Polakoff of Severson & Werson telephone number 415-398-3344 for Defendants Telesis Engineers and Gary Varum.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling in all respect but for post judgment interest. In re: Cross-Complainants ALEC BERGER and NELLY MERZHERITSKY'S Motion For Redetermination Of Amount Of Attorney Fees - Cross-Complainants Alec Berger and Nelly Merzheritsky (the Bergers) are awarded: 1) fees in the amount of $466,744.50 for their prior work and 2) fees in the amount of $12,809.50 for their counsel's work on this motion. Post judgment interest begins as of the date of entry of a written order on this motion. Deductions from the amount awarded by Judge Douglass for the shaded items on exhibits 9 and 10 to the declaration of Adrian Driscoll are warranted since those items do not reflect services performed by counsel for the Bergers in pursuing the Bergers' cross-claims against Cross-Defendants Gary Varum and Telesis Engineers, Inc. (Telesis parties). The Telesis parties' arguments for further deductions lack merit. The services performed by the Ropers firm prior to substituting in as counsel for the Bergers were reasonably incurred on behalf of the Bergers for the Bergers' cross-claims against the Telesis parties. The record on this motion does not support a reduction of 20%, or in any amount, for services performed on the Bergers' claims against Alex Varum and Philip Whitehead since those claims were inextricably connected to the Bergers' claims against the Telesis parties. The court recognizes that the Telesis parties have not had an opportunity to oppose the Bergers' request for fees for this motion since that request was made for the first time in the Bergers' reply brief. The prevailing party is to prepare a form of order.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion By Cross-Complainants For Award Of Attorneys Fees Incurred On Appeal
Off Calendar May-04-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Adrian Driscoll of Murphy Pearosn Bradley & Feeney telephone number 415-788-1900 for Cross-Defendants Alec Berger and Nelly Merzheritskaya.
Adam Polakoff of Severson & Werson telephone number 415-398-3344 for Defendants Telesis Engineers and Gary Varum.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling. In re: CROSS-COMPLAINANTS ALEC BERGER and NELLY MERZHERITSKY'S Motion For Award Of Attorneys Fees Incurred On Appeal - Cross-Complainants Alec Berger and Nelly Merzheritsky (the Bergers) are awarded: 1) fees in the amount of $74,357 for the services performed by their counsel on the appeal and 2) fees in the amount of $7,811.50 for their counsel's work on this motion. Because the Bergers did not prevail on the appeal of the attorney's fees order, a deduction should be made from the fees requested for the work performed by the Bergers' counsel on that issue. In the absence of any more precise way to determine that deduction, the court has allocated one-ninth of the appellate fees requested to that issue because the attorney's fees issue was one of nine issues raised by Cross-Defendants Gary Varum and Telesis Engineers, Inc. (Telesis parties) in their opening appeal brief. The Telesis parties' argument that a 50% reduction is warranted lacks merit since the legal work on the attorney's fees issue was nowhere near 50% of the work expended on the consolidated appeals. All of the fees requested in this motion were reasonably incurred on the consolidated appeals. The court recognizes that the Telesis parties have not had an opportunity to oppose the Bergers' request for fees for this motion since that request was made for the first time in the Bergers' reply brief. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

Notice Of Motion Of Defendant Patrick Mchugh To Set Aside Default Judgment
Off Calendar Jan-22-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Charles Redfield of Low, Ball & Lynch telephone number 415-981-6630 for Moving Party/Defendant Patrick McHugh.
Mark Meyer of Losch & Ehrlich telephone number 415-956-8400 for Judgment Creditor Alec Berger.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT PATRICK MCHUGH DBA MCHUGH CONSTRUCTION'S Motion To Set Aside Default Judgment is n denied. CCP section 473.5 permits the court to set aside a default or default judgment against a defendant, if the defendant shows at least the four following facts in his motion: (1) He received, through no inexcusable fault of his own, no actual notice of the action in time to appear and defend, and had not made a general appearance in the action. (2) A default judgment had been entered against him by the court. (3) He acted with reasonable diligence in serving and filing the notice of motion to set aside the default or default judgment (and in no event was the notice of motion served and filed later than the earlier of (a) two years after the entry of a default judgment against him or (b) 180 days after service on him of written notice of the default or the default judgment). (4) That he has a meritorious defense. (CCP section 473.5 Judicial Council Comments.) Here, Defendant Patrick McHugh has not presented sufficient evidence to establish that he did not receive notice of the lawsuit. Defendant McHugh does not present evidence to support his claim that the service processor's declaration, which was filed with the proof of service of summons, was fraudulent. Additionally, Defendant McHugh did not establish, in his moving papers, that he has a meritorious defense. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/EG)
